sources.name: Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level Formula
sources.note: Sentence length is the primary FK driver. Average words per sentence is the most controllable variable in the formula.

sources.name: Plain Language Act 2010
sources.note: Short sentences as a core plain language requirement. Long sentences are the single most common plain language violation in government documents.

sources.name: Strunk & White, Elements of Style — Rule 13: Omit Needless Words
sources.note: Public domain. A sentence should contain no unnecessary words. Concision is the foundation of readable sentence length.

sources.name: Strunk & White, Elements of Style — Rule 14: Avoid a Succession of Loose Sentences
sources.note: Public domain. Monotone sentence length is its own violation. Short must follow long.

_description: Sentence length is the primary FK driver. Average target: 15-20 words for general output. Crisis target: 10-15 words average. Hard ceiling: 35 words per sentence. Monotone length is its own violation — short sentences must follow long ones.
_critical_rule: Sentence length is checked after paragraph assembly (step 11), not during word selection. Any sentence above the hard ceiling must be rewritten before the page is assembled.

general_output.note: Average of 15-20 words produces FK 6-8 when paired with 1-2 syllable word average. Hard ceiling of 35 words is absolute — no sentence may exceed it regardless of domain.

crisis_operational.note: Crisis output demands shorter sentences. A reader under stress loses the thread of a sentence above 25 words. Hard ceiling is 25 — stricter than the general ceiling.

medical_patient.note: Same as crisis. Patients under stress process at reduced cognitive load.

professional_report.note: Slightly wider range than general. Professional readers can track longer sentences when the structure is clean.

technical_specialist.note: Technical documentation sometimes requires longer sentences to preserve precision. Hard ceiling still applies.

academic_research.note: Academic writing permits longer sentences when they carry precise meaning. Hard ceiling raised to 40 for this domain only.

rules.rule: No sentence may exceed the hard ceiling for its domain.
rules.rationale: Sentences above the hard ceiling are structurally too complex for any reader to process in a single pass. They must be split regardless of content.
rules.action: Split at the nearest logical boundary. Place the main clause in the first sentence. Move subordinate information to the second.

rules.rule: Average sentence length across a paragraph must fall within the domain target range.
rules.rationale: Individual sentences within the target range do not guarantee the paragraph average is correct. Check the average across all sentences in the paragraph.
rules.action: If average is above the ceiling, identify the longest sentences and split them. If average is below the floor, combine two closely related short sentences.

rules.rule: Monotone sentence length is a violation. Short sentences must follow long ones.
rules.rationale: A paragraph of sentences all the same length loses emphasis and becomes mechanical. Rhythm requires variation. A short sentence after a long one signals importance and gives the reader a pause.
rules.example_correct: The northern route passes through three active conflict zones and requires armed escort at any hour of the day or night. Do not use it.
rules.example_incorrect: The northern route is dangerous. It has conflict zones. It needs an escort. It runs day and night.
rules.action: After any sentence of 20+ words, the following sentence must be shorter than 15 words. After any sentence of 30+ words, the following sentence must be shorter than 12 words.

rules.rule: A sentence of fewer than 5 words is permitted only as a deliberate emphasis or direct instruction.
rules.rationale: Very short sentences are powerful when used deliberately. They lose that power if overused. Three or more consecutive sentences under 8 words become choppy and fragmented.
rules.example_not_permitted: Roads are bad. Avoid them. Stay inside. Wait.
rules.action: No more than two consecutive sentences under 8 words. After two short sentences, the next must be longer.

rules.rule: Reduce sentence length before changing word choice when FK grade is too high.
rules.rationale: Sentence length has a larger effect on FK grade than word syllable count in practical plain language writing. Splitting a 30-word sentence into two 15-word sentences reduces FK more efficiently than replacing polysyllabic words.
rules.action: If FK exceeds ceiling: split sentences first. Then check syllable load. Then check for nominalizations and intensifiers.
rules.source: Flesch-Kincaid formula — sentence length as primary FK variable

split_guidance.description: How to split a sentence that exceeds the hard ceiling
steps: 1. Identify the main clause — the most important fact
2. Make the main clause the first sentence — end with a period
3. Move subordinate information to the second sentence
4. Check that both resulting sentences are complete independent clauses
5. Check that the second sentence is shorter than the first
split_guidance.example_before: The convoy, which had been delayed at the northern checkpoint for six hours due to a vehicle inspection conducted by armed personnel who had not been notified of the convoy's clearance, finally departed at 0300.
split_guidance.example_after: The convoy departed at 0300 after a six-hour delay at the northern checkpoint. Armed personnel conducted a vehicle inspection — they had not been notified of the convoy's clearance.
